Chart examplesA few weeks ago I shared FrameUI, an Angular component library I have been building.
FrameUI now has a dedicated Angular charts package. It is shipping as part of the 1.0.0 release. FrameUI can now cover more of the interfaces it was designed for.
Dashboards, admin tools, reports, operational views, and internal products often need charts next to forms, tables, filters, navigation, and overlays. I wanted those charts to feel like part of the same system instead of something visually attached afterwards.
The new charts package
The new package is called @frame-ui-ng/charts.
npm install @frame-ui-ng/foundation @frame-ui-ng/chartsThe chart styles are imported separately:
@import '@frame-ui-ng/charts/styles.css';And the Angular module can be imported from the charts package:
import { FrChartModule } from '@frame-ui-ng/charts';A basic chart looks like this:
<frame-chart
aria-label="Traffic by device"
type="area"
xKey="month"
[data]="trafficData"
[series]="trafficSeries"
/>The first version of the chart package supports:

• Area Charts

• Bar Charts

• Composed Charts

• …and more
There is also support for grouped and stacked bars, horizontal bars, smooth, linear, sharp, and step curves, composed series, legends, tooltips, value formatting, custom colors, empty states, and chart click events.
Why I added charts
FrameUI is mostly focused on application interfaces.
Those interfaces usually contain more than buttons and forms. They often include small pieces of data visualization:

• traffic over time

• revenue or usage by month

• …
You can absolutely use a general-purpose charting library for that. In many cases, that is still the right choice.
But I wanted a first-party option for the common product UI cases. Something that follows the same token system, uses the same visual language, and does not require every dashboard card to be styled from scratch.
How it fits into FrameUI
I did not want charts to become part of the main component package.
Not every application needs data visualization. Some projects only need buttons, forms, overlays, navigation, tables, and feedback components. Pulling chart code into that package would make the boundary less clear.
Still looking for feedback
I am still interested in practical feedback from Angular developers who build real interfaces.
For the chart package, I am especially interested in:

• Does the chart API feel natural in Angular templates?

• Is the data and series model understandable?

• Are the included chart types useful?

• Should the next work focus more on customization or more chart types?

• Do the charts feel like they belong to the same system as the components?
That kind of feedback is still the most valuable thing for the project.
Docs: https://frame-ui.com
GitHub: https://github.com/gamekohl/frame-ui
If you try it, I would be happy to hear what works, what does not, and where the library should go next.
